title: Context Retention — member states two intents at once at the delivery step
defect: When the member says two things at once, the VA handles one and silently drops the other.
summary: Faithful reduction of the UAT-007 call log to the exact turn where the defect fires.
Agent asks 'fax or email?'. Member answers the delivery method AND asks to update
their ZIP in the same breath. The single-intent WorkerResult schema can only carry
PHASE 3B: the understanding decode recovers the dropped ZIP request from the raw
utterance; the resolver accepts the validated slot answer (fax), marks provider_list
stale, acknowledges BOTH the fax and the ZIP-update in one templated reply, and routes
to the ZIP owner (provider_search) to re-resolve the ZIP before delivery. The Phase 1
gate still guarantees no dispatch can occur on the disputed ZIP.
phase_history: Phase 0: both F1 (silent ZIP drop) and F2 (dispatch on disputed ZIP) demonstrated.
Phase 1: F2 closed by the deterministic stale-delivery gate.
Phase 3B: F1 closed — the ZIP request is acknowledged in the SAME turn and the call
routes to update the ZIP and rebuild before delivery; dropped_request_count → 0.

messages.content: Your in-network provider list is ready. Would you like it sent by fax or email?

turns.user: Fax, but I need to update my ZIP code.
turns.note: The ZIP-update request is NOT representable in WorkerResult — only delivery_method is captured; the understanding decode recovers it from the raw utterance.
turns.expected_behavior_target: Acknowledge BOTH: capture delivery_method=fax AND act on the ZIP-update request.
turns.phase3b_behavior: delivery_method=fax accepted; correction_ack acknowledges the fax AND the ZIP update; provider_list marked dirty; routes to provider_search (awaiting zip_code) to re-resolve before delivery; nothing dispatched; dropped_request_count for this turn = 0.

resolved_failures.description: The ZIP-update request was never acknowledged (silent drop).
resolved_failures.assertion: The turn-0 reply acknowledges BOTH the fax and the ZIP update; the call routes to provider_search (awaiting_slot=zip_code) to re-resolve the ZIP; dropped_request_count == 0.

resolved_failures.description: The provider list was dispatched on the disputed ZIP (94107).
resolved_failures.assertion: dispatch_provider_list is never called while provider_list is dirty; the ZIP is re-resolved before any delivery.
